This needs https://github.com/nerdishbynature/octokit.swift/commit/c59f701c9a32447ccac56f264d88d9a6b280bc0b to go green again.

AFAIK cocoa pods uses jazzy and docs will be at http://cocoadocs.org/docsets/NBNRequestKit/0.1.0/. Would it be nice to include code examples? I kind of liked the documentation in the README tbh. But then I am not a fan of documentation either. We should decide on one way and then remove the docs from the README as documenting it twice is not very sufficient.
Looks good, please merge in master so we can see the tests going through before merging this.

I actually started working on this because I wanted to try out jazzy, I didn't realize it gets generated when you push it to cocoapods. 

It's possible to put code examples in the inline docs, which we could do. Another nice thing is that the docs show up when you option click classes and functions in Xcode. 

If you're not a fan of inline docs and would prefer to keep the README style, we can close this. I'm not set on it.
Nice, didn't realise the Xcode alt+click documentation is done automatically. Could you indent the lines so the comments `/**` start on the same indentation spot as the `func` definitions?
Markdown works, too.
Super good, thanks a lot! Will push version 0.5.0 tomorrow then!
Not on my roadmap, but feel free to do a Pull Request ✌️

OK,I will give it a try.
And before that,how can I add this to my OSX project manually?
You could drop the Xcode project into your project and link the framework with your application. http://stackoverflow.com/questions/3352664/how-to-add-existing-frameworks-in-xcode-4

OK,got it.
And thanks for your lightning response speed.Danke:)
Er...still some problems,should I build this project first?or just clone it and drag the entire folder to my project?I'm a little confused.
I addressed your features in #32
Not tomorrow, I am open for PullRequests! Have a look at the stuff other people contributed, it's really easy https://github.com/nerdishbynature/octokit.swift/pull/18
Ok great... That's what I wanted to suppose to you. I'll contribute it. See you for the PR ;-)
Sweeeet 🎉
👍 Looks legit, waiting for Travis.
Furthermore I improved the previous PR #34  a little bit and make the author of the issue not optional, the assignee really optional and added the HTML url to the issue itself. Have fun :smile_cat:
We might need to distinguish that this is for https://github.com/issues and not https://github.com/nerdishbynature/octokit.swift/issues, for e.g.
@nwest Ha, good catch!

@thomaspaulmann Could you maybe change it to be like [repositories](https://github.com/nerdishbynature/octokit.swift/blob/master/OctoKit/Repositories.swift#L55-L58) and could you perhaps add some documentation for the README?
@nwest good one, I will change it and I've already added some documentation in the README
@nwest Does that fit your expectations?

Ah, I wasn't very clear. See here: https://developer.github.com/v3/issues/#list-issues This PR implements `GET /user/issues`. There's also `GET /orgs/:org/issues`, `GET /issues`, and `GET /repos/:owner/:repo/issues`. You don't have to implement the other methods in this PR, we'll just have to differentiate them. 

Maybe add something like "Get issues across owned and member repositories for the authenticated user" to the README?

Ok, I've decided to switch to the request `GET /issues` because it fits best to my use case and will add the missing documentation. Furthermore I can add the other methods/routes as well, but It could be a confusing naming.
Thanks @thomaspaulmann!
Ha, there was no change to the project file?
@pietbrauer oops, I'll patch it.
Have you moved it to all 4 targets? Mac, iOS, tvOS and Watch?
Ah thank you. I still haven't had my coffee this morning.
@pietbrauer I see what you mean. These are just the issues related calls.
😄😄 yep
@pietbrauer good to go?
Looks good! 🤘
@thomaspaulmann could you rebase this and add the files to the project for all of the targets?
I’ve rebased these changes atop master.
Setting a milestone or label on an issue is blocked by nerdishbynature/RequestKit#11.

#11 implements the following calls:

```
GET /repos/:owner/:repo/issues/:number
POST /repos/:owner/:repo/issues
PATCH /repos/:owner/:repo/issues/:number
```
:rocket:
This also conflicts with master now due to #36
Mainly inspired by http://masilotti.com/testing-nsurlsession-input/
Great idea!

Nice, a PR is definitely welcome! I would map the error to the GitHub response, since GitHub is returning a 200 when the truncated tree is shown I would also truncate the response. Can you see how high limited is until it is truncated?

Noticed tests are failing, specifically with this error:

`Test Case '-[OctoKitTests.FollowTests testGetMyFollowers]' started.`
`2016-05-10 02:19:22.967 xctest[66737:1793820] *** Terminating app due to uncaught exception 'NocillaUnexpectedRequest', reason: 'An unexpected HTTP request was fired.`

I'm not familiar with Nocilla, so it's not clear to me what's causing the problem here.
You make a HTTP request that is not stubbed, probably because there is a new parameter in the call. It should also fail locally in Xcode.
Thanks, that makes sense. Will take another look in the light of day.
Ping 📲
We are waiting for the Travis update, I guess?

The RequestKit clone is failing

```
*** Cloning RequestKit
*** Skipped downloading RequestKit.framework binary due to the error:
    "Maximum number of login attempts exceeded. Please try again later."
```
OK, I'm tired and didn't read that correctly. The binary download failed but the clone succeeded.
RequestKit updated with nerdishbynature/RequestKit#22. Build is green so this PR is ready for review.
Sweet :sparkles:
All builds failed with `/Users/travis/build/nerdishbynature/octokit.swift/OctoKit/Configuration.swift:66:47: optional chain has no effect, expression already produces 'String?'`
Isn't this a CocoaPods bug then?
Thanks, that should be fixed now. iOS and tvOS builds are still failing for reasons unrelated to these changes.

I don’t think it is. As far as I can tell, the `~> 0.3.0` in the podspec means an official source distributed through CocoaPods.org is required. But the Swift 2.3 branch requires an external source instead, so the `~> 0.3.0` is misleading and conflicts with the Git/branch requirement in the podfile.

Shouldn't your Podfile look like this then?

``` ruby
pod 'OctoKit', :git => 'https://github.com/nerdishbynature/OctoKit.swift.git', :branch => 'swift-2.3'
pod 'NBNRequestKit', :git => 'https://github.com/nerdishbynature/RequestKit.git', :branch => 'swift-2.3'
```

It doesn’t matter what order the dependencies are listed in the podfile. The podfile and OctoKit.swift podspec will conflict anyways:

```
Pre-downloading: `NBNRequestKit` from `https://github.com/nerdishbynature/RequestKit.git`, branch `swift-2.3`
Pre-downloading: `OctoKit.swift` from `https://github.com/nerdishbynature/octokit.swift.git`, branch `swift-2.3`
[!] Unable to satisfy the following requirements:

- `NBNRequestKit (from `https://github.com/nerdishbynature/RequestKit.git`, branch `swift-2.3`)` required by `Podfile`
- `NBNRequestKit (~> 0.3.0)` required by `OctoKit.swift (0.6.1)`

Specs satisfying the `NBNRequestKit (from `https://github.com/nerdishbynature/RequestKit.git`, branch `swift-2.3`), NBNRequestKit (~> 0.3.0)` dependency were found, but they required a higher minimum deployment target.
```

(The bit about the higher minimum deployment target does appear to be a bug in CocoaPods. I’m seeing this in a project that has the minimum iOS deployment target set to iOS 8.0, which matches RequestKit’s Swift 2.3 branch.)

This is still problematic. Using octokit.swift’s `swift-2.3` branch installs NBNRequestKit 0.3.0, which is not compatible.

Manually specifying NBNRequestKit `:branch => 'swift-2.3'` in the Podfile results in the same error as above:

```
Pre-downloading: `OctoKit.swift` from `https://github.com/nerdishbynature/octokit.swift.git`, branch `swift-2.3`
[!] Unable to satisfy the following requirements:

- `NBNRequestKit (from `https://github.com/nerdishbynature/RequestKit.git`, branch `swift-2.3`)` required by `Podfile`
- `NBNRequestKit (from `https://github.com/nerdishbynature/RequestKit.git`, branch `swift-2.3`)` required by `Podfile`
- `NBNRequestKit (from `https://github.com/nerdishbynature/RequestKit.git`, branch `swift-2.3`)` required by `Podfile`
- `NBNRequestKit (~> 0.3.0)` required by `OctoKit.swift (0.6.1)`
```
Tests are failing because of Homebrew.
Hey, sorry for the delay, hectic times. I still think this is a cocoa pods bug and opened an issue: https://github.com/CocoaPods/CocoaPods/issues/5912
I will release Swift 2.3 and Swift 3.0 releases today.
